# WS2 verifiability report: which (representation, property) sources are real signal

*Generated by `generate_signal.py` on the NegResultDB ADMET source. 2026-06-09. See `README.md` for the gate. No em dashes.*

## The gate

A source is an admissible B-axis head-to-head task only if the property is in the content AND survives a cold split: **cold AUROC >= 0.65 AND shuffled-label selectivity >= 0.10**. Content-feature probe = Morgan r2/2048 + logistic; cold split = Murcko-scaffold GroupKFold(5); `leakage_drop = random - cold` (large = DTI trap).

## Result: 7 / 7 SMILES ADMET endpoints PASS

| endpoint | n | baseline | random AUROC | **cold AUROC** | leakage drop | **selectivity** | gate |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| herg | 3963 | 0.16 | 0.920 | **0.895** | +0.025 | +0.365 | **PASS** |
| cyp3a4 | 9929 | 0.15 | 0.874 | **0.830** | +0.044 | +0.312 | **PASS** |
| cyp2d6 | 6226 | 0.11 | 0.879 | **0.828** | +0.051 | +0.338 | **PASS** |
| ames | 517 | 0.80 | 0.925 | **0.847** | +0.078 | +0.331 | **PASS** |
| solubility | 866 | 0.21 | 0.850 | **0.791** | +0.059 | +0.289 | **PASS** |
| permeability | 2057 | 0.92 | 0.919 | **0.878** | +0.041 | +0.377 | **PASS** |
| clearance | 46958 | 0.19 | 0.796 | **0.746** | +0.050 | +0.242 | **PASS** |

Generated sample set: **22,203 records** across the 7 endpoints (matched + re_notation + scrambled variants; matched capped at 4000/endpoint). Per-endpoint pairs in `admet/<endpoint>/pairs.jsonl`, gate metrics in `admet/<endpoint>/verifiability.json`.

## Reads

- **All seven pass, none is a DTI trap.** The leakage drop (random minus cold) is small everywhere (0.025 to 0.078), so the signal is not analog leakage that collapses on a scaffold split. Selectivity is high (+0.24 to +0.38), so the probe reads real structural signal, not label noise. Each is a valid (SMILES, content-property) head-to-head task.
- **The ceiling spans a useful range** (cold 0.75 to 0.90), which is what the B-axis wants: the head-to-head gap is only interpretable where the ceiling is high, and a spread of ceilings lets the regime read (expression-dominant vs encoding-weak) be a comparison, not a single number. herg and cyp3a4 are the two already carried into the WS1-B head-to-head; the other five are now gated and ready.
- **Two endpoints are class-imbalanced** (ames baseline 0.80, permeability 0.92). AUROC is robust to base rate, but a balanced subsample (as the activation arm uses) is the right form for the head-to-head; flagged so the gap is read on a balanced set, not the raw skew.
- **Honest scope.** This gates SMILES, the SFM-favorable modality. The reported probe-minus-LLM gap is therefore an upper-bound estimate, not representative across biology. The sequence/variant/metabolite rungs gate the same way with an SFM-embedding featurizer on GPU (see `README.md`); a domain that FAILS the gate means "no measurable signal under this encoder", not "no signal".

## What this gives WS3

WS3 needs a training signal for the read-out PoC (P2). These gated, leakage-controlled pairs are that signal: an expression-limited endpoint (herg, where the LLM encodes ~0.78 internally but outputs ~0.45) is the cleanest target to train a read-out against and measure whether generated output rises toward the encoding ceiling. The gate guarantees the target is real before any training is spent on it.
